behavior_summary: This script implements the in-world behavior for an object/NPC associated with 'Mordea the Tempest'. It provides a context-sensitive short description when the player examines the entity (U8P_MORDEABE::look), updates the visible sprite/frame when an actor enters the trigger area (U8P_MORDEABE::enterFastArea), runs a staged "wake/attack" cutscene when the player uses the object/NPC (U8P_MORDEABE::use), and contains a helper that spawns lightning visuals and applies hit/damage effects (U8P_MORDEABE::func04C0).

U8P_MORDEABE::look(): Chooses one short description/bark based on the item's displayed frame and persistent name/job flags (shows sleeping text if in the sleeping frame, otherwise composes variants using mordeaName and mordeaJob).

U8P_MORDEABE::enterFastArea(): When an actor enters the configured fast/trigger region, set the item's frame to an "awake/active" frame (0x0A) only if mordeaSchedule==0x01 and the referenced NPC is alive; otherwise set a sleeping/inactive frame (0x0B).

U8P_MORDEABE::use(): Guarded by Item::getFrame(this)==0x0A. Freezes the avatar briefly, spawns camera-follow and short-lived timing processes to sequence animations, teleports and animates the NPC, emits several barks (dialog lines), animates the item's display frames in a loop, invokes U8P_MORDEABE::func04C0 to produce lightning visuals and apply damage, and then restores/reset the item frame at the end.

U8P_MORDEABE::func04C0(): Creates temporary effect items, plays a SFX, animates them across several frames with short spawn/wait ticks, spawns/waits on a LightningBolt process and calls Item::receiveHit (using the target and likely the NPC's HP as the magnitude) to deliver damage, then destroys temporary items and returns.
notable_control_flow: U8P_MORDEABE::look() branches by Item::getFrame(this) == 0x0B first (sleeping bed text); otherwise it checks persistent flags mordeaName and mordeaJob to pick among 'Mordea the Tempest', 'Mordea', 'Tempest', or a generic 'sleeping woman' text.
U8P_MORDEABE::enterFastArea() does a two-condition guard: mordeaSchedule == 0x01 AND Npc::isDead([SP-00h]) == false -> set frame 0x0A; else set frame 0x0B.

U8P_MORDEABE::use() is gated by Item::getFrame(this)==0x0A; if false the U8P_MORDEABE::use is ignored. When allowed it runs a sequenced set of spawns + suspend ticks to animate frames down and up using a local iteration counter, with explicit calls to spawn cameraFollowAvatar and many small delay helpers to produce timing.

U8P_MORDEABE::func04C0() runs two distinct visual/damage phases: (1) spawn and animate a first temporary item (frames 0..4) with SFX, (2) spawn/animate a second temporary item (frames 0..9), call Item::receiveHit on the target, wait for LightningBolt process completion, then clean up.
open_questions: Exact numeric semantics of mordeaSchedule: inferred to select awake vs sleeping schedule when equal to 0x01, but what real-world schedule or game-time mapping does that flag represent?

Precise damage semantics: U8P_MORDEABE::func04C0 appears to U8P_MORDEABE::use Npc::getHp() as a value passed to Item::receiveHit — is this intended as an instant-kill/HP-based effect or as a different magnitude transformation?

Which concrete NPC reference is passed on the stack to U8P_MORDEABE::func04C0 (caller-supplied target slot)? The code expects a target at a caller stack slot but the exact source is not explicit in the snippet.

Frame index meanings beyond 0x0A and 0x0B: the animation loop uses a range of frames (0..9 etc.) whose visual mapping to spritesheets isn't present here; exact visuals need art/sprite docs to confirm.

Whether any global flags are toggled by these actions beyond read-only checks (no writes detected here) — could other scripts observe side-effects from teleport/receiveHit that alter global state indirectly?

main_function.name: U8P_MORDEABE::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description/bark shown to the player when they examine the item/NPC. Chooses wording based on the item's frame (sleeping vs awake) and persistent flags indicating personal name and job/epithet.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or examines the object/NPC.
Checks Item::getFrame(this) to detect the sleeping frame (0x0B) before considering name/job flags.
Reads persistent flags mordeaName and mordeaJob to decide phrasing.

function_map.name: U8P_MORDEABE::look
function_map.role: Look/description handler that selects a short description/bark based on item frame and mordeaName/mordeaJob flags.

function_map.name: U8P_MORDEABE::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry behavior: set visible frame (awake vs sleeping) depending on mordeaSchedule and whether the NPC is alive.

function_map.name: U8P_MORDEABE::use
function_map.role: Use interaction: staged cutscene/animation sequence that freezes avatar, focuses camera, animates NPC and item frames, emits barks, teleports NPC, and invokes the lightning/damage helper.

function_map.name: U8P_MORDEABE::func04C0
function_map.role: Utility effect: spawn visual items/processes for a lightning/impact effect, play SFX, animate temporary items, optionally call Item::receiveHit to apply damage, and clean up visuals.

internal_calls.purpose: Trigger the lightning visual + damage subroutine as part of the U8P_MORDEABE::use() cutscene.

external_calls.purpose: Query the item's current display frame to pick an appropriate short description (sleeping vs other states).
Read persistent flags to determine whether to include the personal name and/or job title in the U8P_MORDEABE::look description.
Check the referenced NPC life state before choosing to show the awake frame; avoid showing awake/active state for dead NPCs.
Set the visible frame to awake (0x0A) or sleeping (0x0B) depending on schedule and NPC death check.
Guard: ensure the U8P_MORDEABE::use sequence only proceeds when the item is in the awake/usable frame (0x0A).
Temporarily freeze the player's avatar to prevent interruption during the cutscene/sequence.
Spawn a camera-follow process centered near the item/avatar to stage the cutscene focus.
Animate or rotate the NPC/object during the U8P_MORDEABE::use sequence (directional/pose changes spawned as separate processes).
Spawn short lived timing/delay helper processes used as small waits between frame updates and animation steps.
Move the NPC to specific coordinates as part of the staged animation/cutscene.
Create and place temporary visual items for the lightning/impact effect and clean them up after animation.
Play a specific sound effect (SFX 0x00C8) to accompany the lightning/impact visuals.
Animate the temporary effect items across several frame indices to produce the visual animation.
Apply a hit/damage to the target NPC; inferred to U8P_MORDEABE::use Npc::getHp() as the magnitude and damage flags/types provided in the call.
Spawn a LightningBolt process used/coordinated with the visual items and damage application; U8P_MORDEABE::func04C0 waits for it to complete.

reads.name: mordeaName
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether to include the personal name 'Mordea' in the U8P_MORDEABE::look() description; read by U8P_MORDEABE::look().

reads.name: mordeaJob
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether to include the job/epithet 'Tempest' in the U8P_MORDEABE::look() description; read by U8P_MORDEABE::look().

reads.name: mordeaSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Schedule/state flag used by U8P_MORDEABE::enterFastArea() to choose awake (0x01) vs sleeping frame; read-only here.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-world behavior for an object/NPC associated with 'Mordea the Tempest'. It provides a context-sensitive short description when the player examines the entity (U8P_MORDEABE::look), updates the visible sprite/frame when an actor enters the trigger area (U8P_MORDEABE::enterFastArea), runs a staged "wake/attack" cutscene when the player uses the object/NPC (U8P_MORDEABE::use), and contains a helper that spawns lightning visuals and applies hit/damage effects (U8P_MORDEABE::func04C0).
